BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after Walter mentioned booth fees might rise at the guild shows. Pre-Ted review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Need to revisit with Ted once 2025 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_FeesUp.Label: Booth and marketplace fees rise to $750/mo if show costs pass through
Scenario_A_FeesUp.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/order, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 orders minimum.
Scenario_A_FeesUp.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 orders.

Scenario_B_FeesUpMore.Label: Worst case - fees to $850/mo (unlikely per Walter)
Scenario_B_FeesUpMore.Impact: Still fine. Walter said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Booth and marketplace fees rise to $750/mo if show costs pass through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/order, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 orders minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 orders.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - fees to $850/mo (unlikely per Walter)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Walter said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~28-30 orders at current cost structure. At 55 orders we have plenty of headroom. Main concern was seasonal dip but even at 48 (Dec low) we're safe.
ActionItems: Wait for Walter to confirm the show booth fee situation
Revisit after year-end with Ted for proper modeling
